Anthropic's current focus is Claude Code's rapid iteration cadence and the expansion of the platform's agent infrastructure.

**Claude Opus 4.7 launched as the new flagship model.** Available at the same $5 / $25 per MTok as Opus 4.6, with stronger coding, vision, and long-horizon agentic performance. Opus 4.7 includes API breaking changes versus Opus 4.6 — review the migration guide before upgrading. Claude Sonnet 4 and Opus 4 retire 2026-06-15; migrate to the 4.6 line. Claude Haiku 3 was retired in April. A new `xhigh` effort level for Opus 4.7 sits between `high` and `max`, accessible via `/effort`, `--effort`, or the model picker.

**Claude Code 2.1.x shipped a sustained run of developer-facing capabilities.** Highlights: vim visual mode (`v`/`V`), `/usage` merging `/cost` and `/stats`, custom themes via `/theme`, `--from-pr` support for GitLab / Bitbucket / GitHub Enterprise, per-platform native binaries replacing bundled JS, `sandbox.network.deniedDomains`, Linux PID-namespace subprocess isolation, `claude ultrareview` for CI-ready parallel code review, and a push-notification tool for mobile Remote Control. MCP OAuth handling received multiple rounds of fixes. `alwaysLoad` on MCP server config skips tool-search deferral for specific servers.

**Platform agent stack expanded with Managed Agents, `ant` CLI, memory, and the advisor tool.** Managed Agents (`managed-agents-2026-04-01` beta) provides a hosted harness with sandboxing and SSE streaming. Memory for Managed Agents reached public beta in April. The advisor tool pairs a fast executor with a higher-intelligence guide at near-advisor quality. The `ant` CLI supports YAML resource versioning and native Claude Code integration.

**Claude Design launched under Anthropic Labs.** A new collaborative surface for visual outputs — designs, prototypes, slides, and one-pagers — as Labs's first product. Claude Cowork reached GA on macOS/Windows with OpenTelemetry support, Analytics API access, and SCIM-driven role-based access controls.

**1M token context and 300k output cap reached GA.** Opus 4.6 and Sonnet 4.6 support 1M context at standard pricing with no beta header; media cap raised to 600 images/PDF pages. Message Batches `max_tokens` is now 300k via the `output-300k-2026-03-24` beta header. The Models API now exposes `max_input_tokens`, `max_tokens`, and a `capabilities` object.
